explanation: The sentence is complex, involving ongoing monitoring (continual assessment), a conditional branch (if not able, explain), and multiple conditions. First, 'continually assess its health' maps to globally (G), and 'monitorHealth' can serve as the checked property. 'Assess if it is able to undertake the task' suggests checking health thresholds, represented as health_lessThan_threshold (meaning health is below acceptable). If 'it decides it is not able to' (i.e., health_lessThan_threshold is true), the robot should 'explain why not,' which can be mapped as (health_lessThan_threshold -> explain). All of this should happen continuously (globally), and its health check is repeated (G monitorHealth). So, the final formula is: G(monitorHealth) & G(health_lessThan_threshold -> explain).

explanation_dict.natural_language: continually assess its health
if health is below the acceptable level, provide an explanation

explanation:
The sentence describes several behaviors:
1. The robot should continually assess its health and performance. We interpret this as 'monitorHealth' should be globally true (always running), so G(monitorHealth).
2. The robot should assess if it is able to undertake the task. We interpret continual self-assessment as part of 'monitorHealth'.
3. If it decides it is not able to (if health is below threshold), it should explain why not. This is an implication: if health_lessThan_threshold holds, then explain should follow eventually (as soon as possible after the condition is noticed), so G(health_lessThan_threshold -> F(explain)).
The specification puts both properties together with logical AND (&).
Hence, the final LTL formula is:G(monitorHealth) & G(health_lessThan_threshold -> F(explain))

Therefore, we express: (1) monitorHealth happens always: G(monitorHealth), (2) if the health is less than a threshold, the robot must eventually explain: G(health_lessThan_threshold -> F explain). The full specification is the conjunction (AND) of these properties: G(monitorHealth) & G(health_lessThan_threshold -> F explain).

The sentence requests the robot to always (globally) monitor its health/performance and, if it determines (assesses) that it cannot undertake the task due to health being below threshold, it must provide an explanation. This can be captured in LTL as: 
1. Always monitor health: G(monitorHealth).
2. If the robot's health drops below the threshold, it should eventually (promptly) provide an explanation: G(health_lessThan_threshold -> F explain).
3. We combine both requirements into a single formula by conjunction (&).

Next, 'assess if it is able to undertake the task' can be modeled as a precondition check, but since assessing is implicit in continual monitoring, the next temporal event is when the robot finds its health is below threshold ('health_lessThan_threshold'). If this condition is true, then 'it should explain why not', i.e., provide an explanation the next moment or immediately. We model this as: whenever globally, if the health falls below threshold, the robot explains (explain) (G(health_lessThan_threshold -> F explain)). These requirements are combined with AND.

We are asked to express: (1) the robot should continually assess its health and performance; (2) it should assess if it is able to undertake the task; and (3) if it decides it is not able to, it should explain why not. We only have the atomic propositions 'explain', 'health_lessThan_threshold', and 'monitorHealth'.

2. 'Assess if able to undertake the task' is not given as an explicit atomic proposition, but we can represent unfitness as 'health_lessThan_threshold'.
3. 'If it is not able to (health_lessThan_threshold), it should explain why not (explain)'. This is a temporal implication: G(health_lessThan_threshold -> F explain).

The full formula is G(monitorHealth) & G(health_lessThan_threshold -> F explain).
